relocation.package_summary: OpenAI typically offers a lump-sum relocation allowance rather than full broker-managed moves for most mid-to-senior roles. Amounts vary by level and distance; Seattle-to-SF Bay Area moves commonly receive $25k–$45k lump sum covering movers, temporary housing, flights, and lease-break penalties. Some roles include a separate sign-on relocation bonus.
relocation.policy_notes: Lump sum is generally taxable (not grossed-up in most reported cases). No standard public info on cooldown or vesting cliffs specifically tied to relo bonus. Most relo support is one-time. Sources indicate policy has remained relatively consistent through 2025.

benefits.healthcare: Premium PPO plans via Anthem or Cigna (varies by location), majority of premium covered by employer for employee + dependents, broad national network
benefits.dental_vision: Comprehensive coverage through Delta Dental and VSP or equivalent; employer pays majority of premium
benefits.estimated_copay: ~$150–$350/month for family coverage at senior base levels (after subsidy)
benefits.meals_provided: Breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks provided in-office (San Francisco & other major hubs); Seattle office has more limited catering
benefits.mental_health: Modern Health as primary provider; 12–16 sessions/year covered, includes dependents
benefits.other_perks: Learning stipend ($2k–$5k/yr typical), generous parental leave (20+ weeks), commuter benefits, fitness reimbursement. No formal sabbatical policy reported.

sentiment.blind_score: 3.2/5 – High pay and mission pull praised; recurring complaints about burnout, scope creep on deployment teams, and post-2024 reorganization uncertainty
sentiment.glassdoor_score: 3.9/5 – Strong mission & comp; recurring themes of intense pace, unclear career ladders, and leadership communication issues in 2025–2026 reviews
sentiment.reddit_pulse: Mixed in r/MachineLearning and r/cscareerquestions. Recent threads note high attrition on pilot/deployment teams; one March 2026 comment: “Deployment Manager role is basically on-call firefighter with director-level stress but staff-level pay.” Another thread highlighted better WLB on the Pilots side vs. core research.
sentiment.x_pulse: Recent founder/exec posts emphasize rapid deployment wins and customer pilots; limited direct culture commentary. Some ex-employees on X highlight improved focus post-reorg but note continued high intensity.

likely_recruiter.name: unknown
likely_recruiter.rationale: Search LinkedIn: OpenAI recruiter OR sourcer + 'AI Deployment' OR 'Pilots' OR 'Forward Deployed' OR 'Solutions Architect' (Seattle or Bay Area)

likely_hiring_manager.name: unknown
likely_hiring_manager.rationale: Search LinkedIn: OpenAI 'Head of Pilots' OR 'Director of AI Deployment' OR 'Deployment Manager' manager (filter by 'OpenAI' + 'Pilots' title)
